sourceMovie.title: The Score
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Score for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Frank Oz keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Heist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Score clicked for you, Heist (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Score?
faq.answer: Try Heist, Flawless, Play Dirty—each shares DNA with The Score while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Score?
faq.answer: The Score (2001) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller, Mystery, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Score worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
